units.title: Reusable ops component kit + interaction primitives (FormField/DataTable/Modal/Money/StatusBadge/ModuleGate/EmptyState/AsyncButton + useAsync/useToast)
units.dataPath: Pure presentational + interaction primitives on the existing Tailwind utilities (.btn-primary/.form-input/.form-input-error/.form-label/.form-error in src/index.css). No data path. ModuleGate takes moduleKey + an injected modules map (prop, default from useModules() added by INT-AUTH) and renders children or a locked fallback (Layer C gating, PLATFORM_ARCHITECTURE.md §4.3). Money formats numeric -> USD. useAsync is the idle/pending/success/error state machine every form uses.
units.userFlow: Not a screen. Root dependency: every UI slice imports FormField (label+error+required), DataTable (columns+rows+loading+empty+row actions), Modal (open/onClose/focus-trap), Money, StatusBadge, AsyncButton (pending/disabled/error), ModuleGate (hide/lock when module off), useAsync, useToast.
units.tests: src/components/ops/kit/kit.test.tsx (// @vitest-environment jsdom, renderWithRouter): (a) DataTable renders rows, invokes a row-action onClick with the row object, shows empty state when rows=[] and loading state; (b) ModuleGate renders children when injected map has the key true, renders locked fallback (not children) when false; (c) AsyncButton/useAsync: a rejecting fn flips to error branch, a resolving fn to success; (d) Money formats 15000 -> "$15,000.00"; (e) FormField shows error text. Static audit: no empty onClick, every interactive prop invoked in a test.

units.title: INTEGRATION: api.ts core CRM/contracts/billing wrappers (contacts/clients/horses/engagements/documents/signatures/deliveries/transactions/billable_lines/templates + counts)
units.dataPath: APPENDS to existing src/lib/api.ts (serial head of the api.ts chain; MUST NOT re-author the already-present myModules/orgPublicConfig/configValue/provisionTenant, lines 307-369). Adds: listContacts/createContact/updateContact, listClients, listHorses/createHorse/updateHorse + listHorseBreeds/listHorseColors, listEngagements/getEngagement, listContractTemplates, generateDocument(engId,tmplKey)->rpc('generate_document',{p_engagement_id,p_template_key}), getDocument/listDocuments, recordSignature(docId,role,name,ip)->rpc('record_signature',{p_document_id,p_party_role,p_typed_name,p_ip}), listSignatures, listDeliveries/recordDelivery, listTransactions/getTransaction, listOpenBillableLines/settleBillableLines->rpc('settle_billable_lines',{p_payer_contact_id,p_period}), listIntake, count* helpers. New shared types in src/lib/ops/types.ts. All over supabase.from(...)/supabase.rpc(...) matching tested backbone signatures.
units.userFlow: Not a screen. The single core wiring layer so every core ops/portal slice imports a REAL, exported, RLS-enforced function. Runs serially before feature slices consume it.
units.tests: src/lib/api.ops-core.test.ts (NOT jsdom; mocked supabase client, §15.1): each wrapper invokes supabase.rpc('generate_document',{p_engagement_id,p_template_key}) / rpc('record_signature',{p_document_id,p_party_role,p_typed_name,p_ip}) / rpc('settle_billable_lines',{p_payer_contact_id,p_period}) or the correct .from(table).insert/select with the right columns, returns/throws correctly. Static audit: every exported fn imported by >=1 slice; no defined-but-uncalled RPC; existing 168 tests + typecheck/lint/build stay green (additive).

units.title: INTEGRATION: AuthContext bridge (expose already-loaded modules + role/orgId/isSuperAdmin) + useModules/useEntitlements hooks
units.dataPath: AuthContext.loadProfile ALREADY calls myModules() and holds a `modules` state (fail-closed to [] on error) and projects role/org_id off ProfileRow. This unit SURFACES modules/role/orgId/isSuperAdmin on the context value object (currently only isAdmin/isMember are exposed) and adds useModules()/useEntitlements() hooks reading the context (Layer C, §4.3). Small edit -- the myModules() rpc('my_modules') call already exists; without exposing it, ModuleGate/nav gating silently no-ops (§12).
units.userFlow: Not a screen. The entitlement bridge every ModuleGate/gated nav/route reads. Serial (shared AuthContext.tsx).
units.tests: useModules.test.tsx (jsdom): mock supabase.rpc('my_modules')->['mod.brokerage','mod.lessons']; render a probe under the provider, assert useModules() returns {mod.brokerage:true,mod.boarding:false,...} and role/orgId are surfaced; error path -> modules default to none (fail-closed). Static audit: my_modules rpc called; existing AuthContext consumers (isAdmin/isMember) unchanged and green.

units.title: INTEGRATION: api.ts module + admin wrappers (brokerage/lessons/boarding/barnops/records/employees + module/registry/branding/products)
units.dataPath: APPENDS to src/lib/api.ts AFTER INT-API-CORE (serial; dependsOn is the only shared-file collision guard). Adds: createPurchase/Search/LeaseEngagement->rpc('create_purchase_engagement'|'create_search_engagement'|'create_lease_engagement'), list/createEngagementStage, boarding (facilities/stalls/board_agreements/board_charges), barnops (resources/resource_lots/consumption_events/cost_allocation_rules + resolveConsumptionBilling->rpc('resolve_consumption_billing',{p_period})), lessons (packages/credits/bookings), records (horse_parties/health_events), employees (staff/shifts/time/assignments), admin (setOrgModule->rpc('set_org_module',{p_org,p_key,p_enabled,p_source}), businessConfig, configValues + configRequiredMissing, branding + storage upload, products/prices), listTiers/listModuleCatalog. Exact tested backbone signatures.
units.userFlow: Not a screen. The module/admin wiring layer; runs after INT-API-CORE so it appends with no concurrent write. Every module/admin slice imports from here.
units.tests: src/lib/api.ops-modules.test.ts (mocked supabase client): each wrapper invokes the correct rpc('create_purchase_engagement'|'create_search_engagement'|'create_lease_engagement'|'resolve_consumption_billing'|'set_org_module') with exact p_-args, or the correct .from(table) op. Static audit: no defined-but-uncalled RPC, every export imported by a slice; 168 tests + typecheck/lint/build green.

units.title: Portal kit barrel + typed fixtures (re-exports KIT + hooks; ships portal fixtures)
units.dataPath: No RPC. Re-exports the KIT surface (FormField/DataTable/Modal/Money/StatusBadge/ModuleGate) + useModules/useEntitlements (INT-AUTH) under a single portal-local barrel so thin portal screens import from one place; ships typed fixtures (fake engagement row, document row with merged_body, horse_parties row, billable_lines rows, org_public_config jsonb) mirroring real column shapes.
units.userFlow: Internal: gives every portal/public slice a stable import + fixture seam so they stay thin and testable without re-declaring KIT types.
units.tests: kit-contract.test.tsx (jsdom): render a trivial component using each re-exported primitive (Money, StatusBadge, DataTable with a fixture row) and assert it renders the fixture value; assert the barrel re-exports the expected named hooks (typeof === 'function'). Static audit: no primitive re-exported that KIT does not provide.

units.title: Ops home dashboard (entitlement-aware KPI tiles: engagements/documents/intake/billing counts + module launcher)
units.dataPath: Reads useModules() (INT-AUTH) to show only enabled-module tiles; count queries via INT-API-CORE: countOpenEngagements/countPendingIntake/countDraftDocuments/countOpenBillableLines, all RLS-scoped to current_org(). ModuleGate wraps each module tile.
units.userFlow: Staff lands on /app/ops. Sees KPI tiles (open engagements, intake to review, documents awaiting signature, open charges) each linking to its screen. Module launcher shows Brokerage/Lessons/Boarding/BarnOps/Records/Employees tiles -- locked (ModuleGate) for modules the tenant lacks. Clicking a tile navigates.
units.tests: OpsDashboard.test.tsx (jsdom): mock useModules->{mod.brokerage:true,mod.boarding:false} + count fns; assert (a) enabled tiles render as links to the right routes, (b) a disabled-module tile renders locked and does NOT link, (c) count fns called on mount and resolved numbers render, (d) a rejecting count fn renders an inline error not a blank tile. Static audit: every tile is a real Link or gated.

units.title: CRM contacts directory + create/edit contact drawer
units.dataPath: listContacts()/createContact(input)/updateContact(id,patch) (INT-API-CORE) -> supabase.from('contacts') select/insert/update; RLS org boundary + staff access. org_id defaults to current_org() server-side. DataTable + ContactForm (kit FormField).
units.userFlow: Staff opens /app/ops/contacts -> searchable contact list. 'New contact' opens a Modal ContactForm; submit calls createContact and the row appears. Row click opens edit mode -> updateContact.
units.tests: ContactsPage.test.tsx (jsdom): mock listContacts->[rows]/createContact->{id}; assert table rows; open the form, type name+email, submit, assert createContact called with EXACT {name,email,...}, success toast/refresh renders; make createContact reject -> error branch renders, modal stays open. Static audit: form has onSubmit, no console.log-only handler.

units.title: Horses roster + create/edit horse (breed/color lookups)
units.dataPath: listHorses()/createHorse(input)/updateHorse(id,patch) + listHorseBreeds()/listHorseColors() (global lookups) (INT-API-CORE) -> supabase.from('horses')/from('horse_breeds')/from('horse_colors'). RLS org-scoped; org_id defaults server-side.
units.userFlow: Staff opens /app/ops/horses -> roster (name, breed, color, primary owner). 'New horse' Modal HorseForm with breed/color selects from lookups; submit -> createHorse; edit via row click -> updateHorse.
units.tests: HorsesPage.test.tsx (jsdom): mock list/create + lookup fns; assert breed/color selects render options from the lookups; submit form -> createHorse called with correct args; success renders new row; reject -> error branch. Static audit: selects wired, submit real.

units.title: Engagements list + detail (parties/horse/transaction/stages/documents rollup)
units.dataPath: listEngagements(filter)/getEngagement(id) (INT-API-CORE) -> supabase.from('engagements') with joined engagement_parties, primary horse, transactions, documents (all RLS org-scoped). StatusBadge on status; Money on amount. Detail reads documents for the engagement and links to generate/viewer/sign slices.
units.userFlow: Staff opens /app/ops/engagements -> filterable table. Row -> /app/ops/engagements/:id detail: parties, horse, transaction, stages, and a Documents section (list + 'Generate document' -> OPS-DOC-GEN, each doc -> OPS-DOC-VIEW).
units.tests: EngagementsPage.test.tsx + EngagementDetailPage.test.tsx (jsdom): mock listEngagements/getEngagement; assert table renders + status badges; row click navigates (route param), detail calls getEngagement(id) with the URL id; parties/horse/txn render, Documents section lists docs; reject -> error state. Static audit: 'Generate document' is a real link/handler.

units.title: Generate-document modal (template picker -> generate_document -> draft created)
units.dataPath: listContractTemplates() (global contract_templates, world-read) + generateDocument(engagementId, templateKey)->rpc('generate_document',{p_engagement_id,p_template_key}) returning {document_id, merged_body} (config keyed to v_eng.org_id -- tenant-correct). Draft document row lands in documents (status DRAFT).
units.userFlow: From an engagement detail, staff clicks 'Generate document' -> Modal lists templates (key+title) -> pick one -> generateDocument(engagementId, templateKey) -> success shows the returned document_id/preview link and closes to the viewer (OPS-DOC-VIEW).
units.tests: GenerateDocumentModal.test.tsx (jsdom): mock listContractTemplates->[templates]/generateDocument->{document_id,merged_body}; render with engagementId prop; assert templates render; pick + confirm -> generateDocument called with (engagementId, chosen templateKey) EXACTLY; success surfaces document_id; reject -> error branch, modal stays open. Static audit: confirm calls the real rpc, no default templateKey silently sent.

units.title: Merged-contract viewer (read-only rendered body, status, parties, effective date, signature roster)
units.dataPath: getDocument(id) (INT-API-CORE) -> supabase.from('documents') select merged_body,status,title,effective_date + joined engagement + signatures (RLS org-scoped + caller_owns_document for clients; staff sees all in-tenant). Read-only render; StatusBadge on document_status.
units.userFlow: Staff opens /app/ops/documents/:id -> merged contract body (tenant-correct tokens already merged), title, status, effective date, signature roster (who has/hasn't signed) with a link to the signing panel (OPS-DOC-SIGN).
units.tests: DocumentViewerPage.test.tsx (jsdom): mock getDocument->{merged_body,status,signatures}; render at :id, assert getDocument called with URL id, merged_body text renders, StatusBadge shows status, signature rows render (signed vs pending); reject -> error page. Static audit: no editable field on a read-only viewer; sign link is real.

units.title: Multi-party staff-facilitated signing panel (record_signature per party -> EXECUTED)
units.dataPath: recordSignature(documentId, partyRole, typedName, ip?)->rpc('record_signature',{p_document_id,p_party_role,p_typed_name,p_ip}) (seals the signature; when all required parties sign, documents.status -> EXECUTED). listSignatures(documentId) refreshes the roster.
units.userFlow: On the viewer, staff facilitates assisted signing: for each unsigned party role, staff types the signer's name and clicks Sign -> recordSignature(documentId, party_role, typed_name). The row seals. When the last required party signs, the doc flips to EXECUTED and the executed banner shows.
units.tests: SigningPanel.test.tsx (jsdom): mock recordSignature->'SIGNED'/status + listSignatures; render with unsigned parties; type a name for a party, click Sign, assert recordSignature called with (documentId, that party_role, typed_name) EXACTLY; success re-renders that row signed; last-party call resolves to EXECUTED -> executed state renders; reject -> error branch, row stays unsigned. Static audit: each Sign button wired to its own role (no shared-role bug), no no-op sign.

units.title: Document delivery panel (record delivery on EXECUTED -- completes the contract chain)
units.dataPath: listDeliveries(documentId)/recordDelivery({document_id,channel,recipient}) (INT-API-CORE) -> supabase.from('document_deliveries') select/insert (RLS org-scoped) for an EXECUTED document. Part of §15 critical chain 2 tail.
units.userFlow: After a document is EXECUTED, staff records/sends delivery (channel: email/portal, recipient) -> recordDelivery; the log shows sent status. Disabled/hidden until EXECUTED.
units.tests: DeliveryPanel.test.tsx (jsdom): mock recordDelivery + listDeliveries; render for an EXECUTED doc, submit a delivery, assert recordDelivery called with {document_id,channel,recipient} exactly and the log row appears; render for a DRAFT doc -> send control disabled/absent; reject -> error branch. Static audit: send button gated on EXECUTED, wired to real insert.

units.title: Documents work-queue (all in-tenant documents by status: DRAFT/SENT/EXECUTED)
units.dataPath: listDocuments(statusFilter) (INT-API-CORE) -> supabase.from('documents') select engagement/title/status/generated_at (RLS org-scoped, staff sees all in-tenant). StatusBadge; links each doc to OPS-DOC-VIEW.
units.userFlow: Staff opens /app/ops/documents-queue -> all documents across engagements filtered by status; sort by generated_at; each row links to viewer/signing. Backs OPS-DASH's documents tile.
units.tests: DocumentsQueuePage.test.tsx (jsdom): mock listDocuments; assert rows + status badges; change the status filter -> listDocuments called with the new status arg; row link navigates to /app/ops/documents/:id; reject -> error. Static audit: filter wired to the query, links real.

units.title: Transactions / invoices list + detail (deal txns + INVOICE settlement rows)
units.dataPath: listTransactions(filter)/getTransaction(id) (INT-API-CORE) -> supabase.from('transactions') (org-scoped; txn_type includes INVOICE, payer_contact_id, period, relaxed engagement_id per U17) with joined billable_lines for INVOICE rows and payer contact. Money + StatusBadge.
units.userFlow: Staff opens /app/ops/transactions -> list of PURCHASE/SALE/LEASE/INVOICE rows. Row -> detail: for an INVOICE, the rolled-up billable_lines that composed it; for a deal txn, engagement + deposit/balance. Read/reconcile view.
units.tests: TransactionsPage.test.tsx (jsdom): mock listTransactions/getTransaction; assert rows render with Money + status; open an INVOICE detail, assert getTransaction(id) and the composing billable_lines render summing to amount; a deal txn shows engagement link; reject -> error. Static audit: read-only, links real.

units.title: Shared settlement modal (billable_lines -> transactions INVOICE) -- reused by boarding/lessons/barnops
units.dataPath: listOpenBillableLines(payerContactId, period)/settleBillableLines(payerContactId, period)->rpc('settle_billable_lines',{p_payer_contact_id,p_period}) (INT-API-CORE) -> supabase.from('billable_lines') select where status='OPEN' + the rpc (rolls OPEN lines into one transactions INVOICE, stamps SETTLED, idempotent). Money on line + invoice totals.
units.userFlow: Reused primitive: given a payer + period, lists that payer's OPEN billable_lines summing to a total; 'Create invoice' -> settleBillableLines rolls them into one INVOICE and shows {transaction_id, amount, lines_settled}. Re-open is a no-op (idempotent) -- nothing-to-settle.
units.tests: SettleModal.test.tsx (jsdom): mock listOpenBillableLines->[lines]/settleBillableLines->{transaction_id,amount,lines_settled}; render for a payer+period; assert open lines + summed total (Money); click Create invoice -> settleBillableLines called with (payerContactId, period) EXACTLY; success shows invoice id + amount; a second click with zero open lines shows nothing-to-settle (idempotent); reject -> error branch. Static audit: settle button is the real rpc, total derived from real lines.

units.title: Create-engagement wizard (purchase / search / lease) -- brokerage-gated
units.dataPath: createPurchaseEngagement({p_buyer_contact_id,p_horse_id,p_seller_contact_id,p_amount,p_deposit})/createSearchEngagement({p_client_contact_id,p_retained_by,p_deal_side,p_horse_id})/createLeaseEngagement({p_client_contact_id,p_deal_side,p_horse_id,p_counterparty_contact_id}) (INT-API-MODULES) -> supabase.rpc('create_purchase_engagement'|'create_search_engagement'|'create_lease_engagement') (all require_module('mod.brokerage')). Contact/horse pickers reuse listContacts/listHorses. Whole page wrapped in ModuleGate('mod.brokerage').
units.userFlow: Brokerage-tenant staff opens /app/ops/engagements/new -> picks type. Purchase: buyer, seller, horse, amount, deposit -> createPurchaseEngagement -> navigates to the new engagement. Search: client, retained_by, deal_side. Lease: client, deal_side (LEASE_IN/OUT), counterparty. Lesson-only tenant sees the ModuleGate lock.
units.tests: CreateEngagementPage.test.tsx (jsdom): mock the three rpc wrappers + contact/horse lists + useModules. (a) brokerage ON: fill purchase form, submit, assert createPurchaseEngagement called with EXACT p-shaped args and success navigates with the returned id; repeat minimal args for search+lease asserting correct fn + args; (b) reject -> error branch, no navigation; (c) brokerage OFF -> ModuleGate lock, forms absent. Static audit: each form onSubmit wired to its own rpc; no cross-wiring.

units.title: Engagement stages panel (add SEARCH/EVALUATION/TRANSACTION_REP stage, directional) -- brokerage-gated
units.dataPath: listEngagementStages(engagementId)/createEngagementStage({engagement_id,stage,retained_by,deal_side,fee_value_key}) (INT-API-MODULES) -> supabase.from('engagement_stages') select/insert (boundary + module_gate('mod.brokerage'); each stage independent, no required predecessor). fee_value_key from a config key list. ModuleGate('mod.brokerage').
units.userFlow: On the engagement detail (embedded panel), staff sees stages (stage/direction/status/fee key). 'Add stage' picks stage type + retained_by + deal_side + fee_value_key -> createEngagementStage; the row appears. Off-module: panel locked.
units.tests: StagesPanel.test.tsx (jsdom): mock list/create stage fns; render inside a route with an engagement id; assert existing stages render with StatusBadge; add a stage, assert createEngagementStage called with {engagement_id from prop, stage, retained_by, deal_side} exactly; reject -> error; ModuleGate-off hides the form. Static audit: form wired, direction/stage selects invoked.

units.title: Intake-review inbox (public requests/bookings/inquiries -> convert to engagement)
units.dataPath: listIntake() reading requests + request_selections + inquiries + bookings (org-scoped by U4, RLS current_org()) (INT-API-CORE); convert action ensures a contact (createContact) then calls the matching engagement RPC (createPurchaseEngagement/createSearchEngagement, INT-API-MODULES) and links selection context. ModuleGate('mod.brokerage') only on the convert-to-brokerage action, not the inbox read.
units.userFlow: Staff opens /app/ops/intake -> inbox of public submissions. Row -> detail drawer with full request + selections. 'Convert to engagement' -> creates/links the contact and opens/creates the engagement, then navigates. Handled items drop off the pending view.
units.tests: IntakeInboxPage.test.tsx (jsdom): mock listIntake->[requests]/createContact/createSearchEngagement; assert inbox rows render; open a row, click convert, assert createContact then the engagement rpc called with args derived from the request; success navigates; reject at any step -> error branch, item stays pending. Static audit: convert is a real multi-step handler, no no-op review buttons.

units.title: Boarding: facilities + stalls management (create/edit, occupancy) -- boarding-gated
units.dataPath: listFacilities()/createFacility()/updateFacility() + listStalls(facilityId)/createStall()/updateStall() (INT-API-MODULES) -> supabase.from('facilities')/from('stalls') (boundary + module_gate('mod.boarding')). ModuleGate('mod.boarding').
units.userFlow: Boarding-tenant staff opens /app/ops/boarding/facilities -> facilities list; add/edit a facility; drill into stalls (code/type/active); add/edit stalls. FHE (boarding OFF) sees the lock.
units.tests: FacilitiesPage.test.tsx (jsdom): mock facility/stall list+create fns + useModules. Boarding ON: create a facility -> createFacility with exact args, row appears; create a stall -> createStall with {facility_id,...}; reject -> error. Boarding OFF: ModuleGate lock, forms absent. Static audit: both forms wired.

units.title: Boarding: board agreements (horse-stall-boarder, rate from registry) -- boarding-gated
units.dataPath: listBoardAgreements()/createBoardAgreement({horse_id,stall_id,boarder_contact_id,board_rate,board_type,start_date,end_date})/updateBoardAgreement() (INT-API-MODULES) -> supabase.from('board_agreements') (module_gate('mod.boarding'); board_rate defaults from config_value('BOARDING','DEFAULT_BOARD_RATE') server-side if omitted). Reuses listHorses/listContacts/listStalls pickers; Money on rate. ModuleGate('mod.boarding').
units.userFlow: Staff opens /app/ops/boarding/agreements -> active agreements. New agreement: pick horse+stall+boarder, rate prefilled from registry default (editable), dates -> createBoardAgreement. Edit/end via row.
units.tests: BoardAgreementsPage.test.tsx (jsdom): mock create/list + pickers; submit -> createBoardAgreement with exact contact/horse/stall/rate args; success row appears with Money-formatted rate; omit rate -> assert null sent (server default) not a hardcoded literal; reject -> error. Static audit: pickers + form wired; ModuleGate off-state covered.

units.title: Boarding: period charges -> billable_lines + settle to invoice -- boarding-gated
units.dataPath: listBoardCharges(agreementId)/createBoardCharge({board_agreement_id,period_start,period_end,amount}) (INT-API-MODULES) -> supabase.from('board_charges') insert (deterministic rate x period; emits/links a billable_lines source_kind='board' row). Settlement reuses the shared SettleModal (OPS-SETTLE) -> settle_billable_lines. ModuleGate('mod.boarding').
units.userFlow: Staff opens /app/ops/boarding/charges -> for an agreement, generate a period charge -> createBoardCharge creates the charge + its board billable_line. 'Settle to invoice' (shared SettleModal) rolls the boarder's OPEN lines into a transactions INVOICE.
units.tests: BoardChargesPage.test.tsx (jsdom): mock createBoardCharge/listBoardCharges + settle wrapper; generate a charge -> createBoardCharge with period+amount exactly, row appears; invoke settle -> settleBillableLines with the boarder contact + period; success shows the invoice; reject -> error. Static audit: generate + settle both wired to real fns.

units.title: Inventory: resources catalog + purchase lots (on-hand, unit_cost, vendor) -- barnops-gated
units.dataPath: listResources()/createResource({resource_key,name,category,unit_of_measure,is_consumable}) + listResourceLots(resourceId)/createResourceLot({resource_id,vendor_contact_id,qty_purchased,unit_cost,on_hand,purchased_at}) (INT-API-MODULES) -> supabase.from('resources')/from('resource_lots') (module_gate('mod.barnops')). Money on unit_cost; ModuleGate('mod.barnops').
units.userFlow: Barn-ops-tenant staff opens /app/ops/barnops/resources -> resource catalog. Add a resource; drill in to add purchase lots (vendor, qty, unit cost) that set on_hand. FHE (barnops OFF) sees the lock.
units.tests: ResourcesPage.test.tsx (jsdom): mock resource/lot fns + useModules; create a resource -> createResource with exact args + category select value; create a lot -> createResourceLot with {resource_id,unit_cost,...}; Money renders unit_cost; reject -> error; ModuleGate off-state hides forms. Static audit: both forms wired.

units.title: Inventory: consumption logging (append-only event: resource x horse x qty x staff) -- barnops-gated
units.dataPath: listConsumptionEvents(filter)/logConsumption({resource_id,resource_lot_id,horse_id,qty,administered_by,occurred_at,notes}) (INT-API-MODULES) -> supabase.from('consumption_events') INSERT (append-only; NEVER computes money). Reuses resource/horse/staff pickers. ModuleGate('mod.barnops').
units.userFlow: Staff opens /app/ops/barnops/consumption -> quick log form (resource, optional lot, optional horse, qty, administered_by, occurred_at, notes) -> logConsumption appends the fact. Recent events list (read-only; no edit/delete). Cost attribution is a separate resolve step (OPS-INV-ALLOC).
units.tests: ConsumptionLogPage.test.tsx (jsdom): mock logConsumption/list + pickers; submit -> logConsumption with EXACT {resource_id,horse_id,qty,...}; success prepends the event; assert NO edit/delete control on rows (append-only invariant); reject -> error. Static audit: form wired, no money field on the log.

units.title: Inventory: cost-allocation split UI (horse_parties-derived) + override rules + resolve billing -- barnops-gated
units.dataPath: listCostAllocationRules()/createCostAllocationRule({scope,scope_id,payer_contact_id,share_pct,effective_from,effective_to}) -> supabase.from('cost_allocation_rules'); resolveConsumptionBilling(period)->rpc('resolve_consumption_billing',{p_period}) (returns int count; splits per horse_parties by default, cost_allocation_rules as explicit override, remainder -> default/barn payer, never dropped). Preview reads billable_lines source_kind='consumption'. ModuleGate('mod.barnops').
units.userFlow: Staff opens /app/ops/barnops/cost-allocation -> derived owner/lessee split (from horse_parties) per horse; add an explicit OVERRIDE rule. 'Resolve billing for period' -> resolveConsumptionBilling(period) generates per-payer consumption billable_lines; preview shows per-payer amounts, then 'Settle to invoice' (shared SettleModal).
units.tests: CostAllocationPage.test.tsx (jsdom): mock list/create rule + resolveConsumptionBilling + settle. (a) add override -> createCostAllocationRule with exact {scope,scope_id,payer,share_pct}; (b) resolve -> resolveConsumptionBilling called with the tstzrange, returned count/preview renders; (c) resolve reject -> error; (d) settle -> settleBillableLines with payer+period. Static audit: resolve + override + settle all wired; no fabricated 100%-owner default in the UI.

units.title: Lessons: packages + client credit balances (grant/adjust credits) -- lessons-gated
units.dataPath: listLessonPackages()/createLessonPackage({package_key,name,price_value_key,credits}) + listLessonCredits(clientId)/grantLessonCredits({client_id,package_key,credits_total,credits_remaining}) (INT-API-MODULES) -> supabase.from('lesson_packages')/from('lesson_credits') (module_gate('mod.lessons')). price_value_key from a config-key picker (registry, never a literal). ModuleGate('mod.lessons').
units.userFlow: Lesson-barn staff (FHE has this) opens /app/ops/lessons/packages -> package catalog. Add a package. Drill into a client's credit balance; grant a package's credits -> grantLessonCredits sets total/remaining. Consumption happens at booking (OPS-LESSONS-BOOK).
units.tests: LessonPackagesPage.test.tsx (jsdom): mock package/credit fns + useModules; create a package -> createLessonPackage with exact args incl. a registry price_value_key (assert NO numeric literal in the payload); grant credits -> grantLessonCredits with {client_id,credits}; reject -> error; ModuleGate off-state. Static audit: forms wired; price is a key not a literal.

units.title: Lessons: booking scheduler (consume a credit; optional instructor) -- lessons-gated
units.dataPath: listLessonBookings(filter)/createLessonBooking({client_id,lesson_credit_id,scheduled_at,duration_min,instructor_staff_profile_id,credits_used})/updateLessonBookingStatus(id,status) (INT-API-MODULES) -> supabase.from('lesson_bookings') (module_gate('mod.lessons'); insert trigger decrements lesson_credits.credits_remaining guarding non-negative; COMPLETED may emit a billable_lines source_kind='lesson' overage). instructor_staff_profile_id NULLABLE (works with mod.employees OFF). ModuleGate('mod.lessons').
units.userFlow: Staff opens /app/ops/lessons/bookings -> schedule a lesson: pick client (with a credit balance), scheduled_at, duration, optional instructor -> createLessonBooking consumes a credit. Mark bookings COMPLETED/NO_SHOW/CANCELLED via row -> updateLessonBookingStatus.
units.tests: LessonBookingsPage.test.tsx (jsdom): mock booking fns + useModules; book -> createLessonBooking with client/credit/scheduled args exactly, success row + remaining-credits reflected; instructor omitted -> null sent (employees-off safe); change status to COMPLETED -> updateLessonBookingStatus(id,'COMPLETED'); reject -> error. Static audit: booking + status change wired; no double-decrement in the UI.

units.title: Horse records: ownership/rights ledger (owner/lessee/trainer shares, effective-dated) -- records-gated
units.dataPath: listHorseParties(horseId)/createHorseParty({horse_id,contact_id,role,share_pct,effective_from,effective_to})/endHorseParty(id) (INT-API-MODULES) -> supabase.from('horse_parties') (module_gate('mod.horserecords'); single source of the cost-attribution split read by resolve_consumption_billing). Reuses listContacts. ModuleGate('mod.horserecords').
units.userFlow: Records-tenant staff (FHE has this) opens /app/ops/records/horse-parties?horse=:id -> ownership/rights ledger (owner/lessee/trainer/caretaker/boarder + share_pct + effective dates). Add/end a party. Editing a lease share flows straight into cost allocation (no parallel rule edit).
units.tests: HorsePartiesPage.test.tsx (jsdom): mock list/create/end + contacts + useModules; add an owner at 100% then a lessee at 50% -> createHorseParty called with each exact {role,share_pct,effective_from}; success rows render; end a party -> endHorseParty(id); reject -> error; ModuleGate off-state. Static audit: role/share form wired; shares surfaced for the allocation link.

units.title: Horse records: health events log (vet/farrier/vaccination/coggins, next-due) -- records-gated
units.dataPath: listHealthEvents(horseId)/createHealthEvent({horse_id,event_type,occurred_at,provider_contact_id,next_due,notes,document_id})/updateHealthEvent() (INT-API-MODULES) -> supabase.from('horse_health_events') (module_gate('mod.horserecords'); optional document_id links a core e-sign doc). ModuleGate('mod.horserecords').
units.userFlow: Staff opens /app/ops/records/health?horse=:id -> chronological health log. Add an event (vet/farrier/vaccination/deworming/coggins) with an optional linked document and next-due reminder. Overdue next-due surfaced as a badge.
units.tests: HorseHealthPage.test.tsx (jsdom): mock health fns + useModules; add an event -> createHealthEvent with exact {horse_id,event_type,occurred_at,next_due}; success row + StatusBadge for overdue next_due; reject -> error; ModuleGate off-state. Static audit: form wired, provider/doc links real.

units.title: Employees: staff roster (link profile -> staff_profile, title, pay type) -- employees-gated
units.dataPath: listStaffProfiles()/createStaffProfile({profile_user_id,contact_id,title,pay_type})/updateStaffProfile() (INT-API-MODULES) -> supabase.from('staff_profiles') (module_gate('mod.employees'); UNIQUE(org_id,profile_user_id)). Reuses profile/contact pickers. ModuleGate('mod.employees').
units.userFlow: Operations-tenant staff opens /app/ops/employees/staff -> staff roster. Add a staff record linking an existing profile; edit title/pay type/active. FHE (employees OFF) sees the lock. Backs consumption administered_by and engagement assignment.
units.tests: StaffPage.test.tsx (jsdom): mock staff fns + useModules; create -> createStaffProfile with exact {profile_user_id,title,pay_type}; success row; duplicate -> error branch surfaces (UNIQUE); ModuleGate off-state hides forms. Static audit: form wired.

units.title: Employees: shifts, time entries & service assignments -- employees-gated
units.dataPath: createShift({staff_profile_id,starts_at,ends_at,role})/listShifts + createTimeEntry({staff_profile_id,clock_in,clock_out,minutes,source_kind,source_id})/listTimeEntries + createServiceAssignment({engagement_id,staff_profile_id,service_type,scheduled_at})/listServiceAssignments (INT-API-MODULES) -> supabase.from('shifts')/from('time_entries')/from('service_assignments') (module_gate('mod.employees')). ModuleGate('mod.employees').
units.userFlow: Staff opens /app/ops/employees/schedule -> three sub-panels: schedule a shift; log a time entry (clock in/out -> minutes); assign staff to an engagement/service occurrence. Backs engagement assigned_staff and service-time costing.
units.tests: SchedulePage.test.tsx (jsdom): mock the six fns + useModules; create a shift -> createShift with exact args; time entry -> createTimeEntry; assignment -> createServiceAssignment with {engagement_id,staff_profile_id,service_type}; each reject -> its own error branch; ModuleGate off-state. Static audit: three distinct forms each wired to its own fn (no cross-wiring).

units.title: Tenant admin: module & entitlement toggles (set_org_module) -- admin-only
units.dataPath: listModuleCatalog() (global modules) + myModules() (org_modules for current_org) + setOrgModule(orgId, moduleKey, enabled, source)->rpc('set_org_module',{p_org,p_key,p_enabled,p_source}) (INT-API-MODULES; SUPER_ADMIN or billing webhook; for ADMIN self-service the wrapper targets current_org, server enforces authority). StatusBadge on enabled/expires.
units.userFlow: Tenant ADMIN opens /app/ops/admin/modules -> each feature module with an on/off toggle + source + expiry. Toggling calls setOrgModule; on success the nav/route gating (via useModules) updates live. Core modules shown always-on/locked.
units.tests: ModuleTogglesPage.test.tsx (jsdom): mock listModuleCatalog/myModules/setOrgModule; assert each module row with its current enabled state; toggle ON -> setOrgModule called with (orgId, moduleKey, true, source) EXACTLY, success reflects the new state; a permission-denied reject -> error branch, toggle reverts; core modules render locked (no toggle). Static audit: toggle wired to the real rpc, optimistic state reconciled with the result.

units.title: Tenant admin: value-registry editor (business_config typed + config_values EAV, completeness check) -- admin-only
units.dataPath: getBusinessConfig()/updateBusinessConfig(patch) -> supabase.from('business_config') (typed legal/financial, admin-only RLS) + listConfigValues(namespace)/upsertConfigValue({namespace,key,value_text/num/json}) -> supabase.from('config_values') + configRequiredMissing()->rpc('config_required_missing',{p_org}) (flags required-but-unset keys against config_keys). One write propagates to every contract merge/receipt.
units.userFlow: ADMIN opens /app/ops/admin/registry -> typed form for legal/financial values (signatory, commission rates, cancellation/late/no-show fees, tax, protection period) + an EAV editor for the long tail. A completeness banner (config_required_missing) lists required keys still unset. Saving updates the single source every {{ORG.*}}/{{TXN.*}} token resolves from.
units.tests: RegistryEditorPage.test.tsx (jsdom): mock get/update businessConfig + list/upsert configValues + configRequiredMissing->[missing]. (a) edit signatory_name + a commission rate, save -> updateBusinessConfig with the exact patch; (b) upsert a config_value -> upsertConfigValue with {namespace,key,value}; (c) completeness banner renders the missing keys; (d) reject on save -> error branch, values not lost. Static audit: typed + EAV forms both wired; completeness call real.

units.title: Tenant admin: branding editor (BRAND.*/CONTACT.* config_values + logo upload to brand-assets) -- admin-only
units.dataPath: getBranding()/saveBranding({name,short_name,tagline,primary_color,secondary_color,copy...}) -> supabase.from('config_values') upsert ns 'BRAND' + 'CONTACT' + uploadBrandAsset(file) -> supabase.storage.from('brand-assets').upload(`${orgId}/logo...`) (org_id-prefixed policy). Drives BrandProvider + document letterhead + org_public_config.
units.userFlow: ADMIN opens /app/ops/admin/branding -> brand name/short name/tagline/colors/copy + contact email/phone/url fields, and a logo uploader. Save writes BRAND.*/CONTACT.* config_values; upload stores the logo under the org's storage prefix. Changes propagate to the public site + member app brand + contract footers.
units.tests: BrandingEditorPage.test.tsx (jsdom): mock get/save branding + upload (storage client mocked). (a) edit name+primary_color, save -> saveBranding with the exact BRAND/CONTACT payload; (b) select a file -> uploadBrandAsset with the file and an org-prefixed path; success shows the preview; reject -> error branch. Static audit: form + uploader wired; no client-supplied org_id (path derived from the authed org).

units.title: Tenant admin: products & pricing catalog (products + effective-dated product_prices) -- admin-only
units.dataPath: listProducts()/createProduct({product_key,name,service_type,module_key,price_value_key,active})/updateProduct() + listProductPrices(productId)/setProductPrice({product_id,amount,effective_from,effective_to}) (INT-API-MODULES) -> supabase.from('products')/from('product_prices') (org boundary + module_gate(module_key) where the product belongs to a module). Money on price. Active/public products feed org_public_config.
units.userFlow: ADMIN opens /app/ops/admin/products -> per-org sellable SKUs. Add/edit a product; set an effective-dated price (history preserved, snapshotted at sale). Prices reference the registry key, never literals.
units.tests: ProductsPage.test.tsx (jsdom): mock product/price fns; create a product -> createProduct with exact args (incl. module_key gating visibility); set a price -> setProductPrice with {product_id,amount,effective_from}; Money renders the price; reject -> error; toggling active -> updateProduct. Static audit: both forms wired; price effective-dated not overwritten in place.

units.title: Super-admin: provision-a-tenant wizard (name/slug/tier/admin + brand/legal/rates jsonb -> provision_tenant)
units.dataPath: provisionTenant({name,slug,tierKey,adminEmail,brand,legal,rates,modules}) (ALREADY in api.ts) posts to /api/admin-provision-tenant (INT-API-PROVISION) which find-or-creates the ADMIN auth user then rpc('provision_tenant',{p_name,p_slug,p_tier_key,p_admin_email,p_admin_user_id,p_brand,p_legal,p_rates,p_modules}) -> returns the new org_id. listTiers()/listModuleCatalog() populate the pickers.
units.userFlow: SUPER_ADMIN opens /app/ops/superadmin/provision -> wizard: org name + unique slug, tier, ADMIN email, optional add-on modules, brand seed, legal seed, rate seed. Submit -> provisionTenant -> shows the new org id + slug + 'branded site is live at /<slug>'. Idempotent by email/slug.
units.tests: ProvisionTenantPage.test.tsx (jsdom): mock provisionTenant->newOrgId + listTiers/listModuleCatalog. Fill the wizard, submit -> provisionTenant called with EXACT {name,slug,tierKey,adminEmail,brand,legal,rates,modules} payload; success renders the new org id + slug link (ProvisionResult); a duplicate-slug reject -> error branch, form retained; a non-superadmin (mock useModules/role) sees the page gated. Static audit: single submit is the real provision path; brand/legal/rates collected into the payload, no dropped fields.

units.title: 27-form intake schema renderer (FormRenderer core, no submit)
units.dataPath: Reads form_definitions.schema jsonb ({ sections:[{ heading, fields:[{key,label,type,required,options,...}] }] }) fetched by useFormDefinition(form_key) (added to api.ts by INT-API-PORTAL as fetchFormDefinition(form_key) -> supabase.from('form_definitions').select('*').eq('form_key',k).eq('active',true).single()). Pure renderer: schema -> KIT Field controls (text/email/tel/select/textarea/checkbox/date), collects a values object, validates required. Emits values via onSubmit prop -- no direct DB write (submission is CP-INTAKE-2/3).
units.userFlow: Any of the forms renders identically from its jsonb schema: sections + fields draw, required fields block empty submit, values object assembles on submit.
units.tests: FormRenderer.test.tsx (jsdom): render with a fixture schema (2 sections, required text + select), type into a field, select an option, click Submit; assert onSubmit called ONCE with the exact typed values object; a required-empty submit does NOT call onSubmit and renders the field error. Static audit: every field type maps to a real control; no field renders a no-op input.

units.title: Public intake submission (anon, addressed-tenant, slug->org)
units.dataPath: Wraps FormRenderer for the anon public path. On submit calls submitIntake(slug, formValues) -> the real submitRequest/intake path in api.ts (INT-API-PORTAL). Anon public writes resolve org_id from the submitted slug server-side (§5.2/§8.6, never client-supplied); Class-B intake tables keep nullable org_id per §8.6, stamped from the addressed slug. Reads useBrand()/org_public_config(slug) to know the addressed tenant. Tables: requests + request_selections (Class B).
units.userFlow: A prospect on tenant.example.com opens a public intake form -> fills it -> submits -> row lands scoped to THAT tenant (slug->org), confirmation renders; a second tenant's admin cannot see it.
units.tests: PublicIntakeForm.test.tsx (jsdom): mock the api submitIntake fn; render for slug 'fhe', fill required fields, submit; assert submitIntake called with ('fhe', {exact values}); success confirmation renders; mock a rejection -> error branch renders (values retained). Static audit: form has onSubmit, submit button wired, no console.log-only handler.

units.title: Authenticated client intake submission (CLIENT-audience forms in portal)
units.dataPath: Portal-side rendering of CLIENT-audience form_definitions (audience='CLIENT') for a logged-in client. Fetches via useFormDefinition, renders via FormRenderer, on submit persists through submitClientIntake (INT-API-PORTAL; writes to the same requests/intake surface but org_id defaults to current_org() for the authenticated caller, RLS-scoped). Lists available CLIENT forms from form_definitions where active. useAuth mocked in test.
units.userFlow: A logged-in client opens /app/intake, picks a form (e.g. New Client Profile), fills + submits -> saved under their tenant + linked to their contact; success renders; the form list reflects submitted state.
units.tests: Intake.test.tsx (jsdom, useAuth mocked as a client): render, assert the CLIENT-form list renders from a fixture form_definitions array; open one, fill, submit; assert submitClientIntake called with correct (form_key, values); success + error branches render. Static audit: no dead form; the data fn is imported and called.

units.title: My Engagements list (client portal onto the brokerage/engagement layer)
units.dataPath: useEngagement()/listMyEngagements() (INT-API-PORTAL wrapper fetchMyEngagements -> supabase.from('engagements').select(...) RLS-scoped to caller_owns_engagement/client_id=current_client_id()). Renders engagement rows (service_type, status via StatusBadge, engagement_stages when mod.brokerage on). Gated behind ModuleGate('mod.brokerage') via useModules().
units.userFlow: A client opens /app/engagements -> sees only their own engagements with status; clicking one routes to detail (CP-PORTAL-ENG-2). A lesson-only tenant with brokerage off sees the gated empty/hidden state.
units.tests: MyEngagements.test.tsx (jsdom, useAuth+useModules mocked): brokerage ON + fixture list -> rows render with correct status badge + service label; click a card -> navigate called with /app/engagements/:id. brokerage OFF -> ModuleGate hides the surface. Data hook rejects -> error state renders. Static audit: card click wired to navigate, list fetch a real api fn.

units.title: Engagement detail (stages, documents, transactions for the client)
units.dataPath: useEngagement(id) -> fetchEngagement(id) (engagements + engagement_stages + related documents via fetchEngagementDocuments) all RLS-scoped to the caller's client. Renders stage timeline, linked documents (deep-links to CP-PORTAL-DOC flow), and transactions/balance for the engagement. Read-only client surface.
units.userFlow: From My Engagements a client opens one -> sees its stages, its generated contract documents (with sign CTA if unsigned), and the deal's financial summary; can jump to sign a pending document.
units.tests: EngagementDetail.test.tsx (jsdom, useAuth mocked): render at /app/engagements/:id with a fixture engagement+stages+documents; assert stages render in order with correct badges; an unsigned document shows a 'Review & Sign' link routing to the sign flow; a signed/EXECUTED document shows executed state. Fetch rejects -> error renders. Static audit: sign link is a real route, no dead buttons.

units.title: My Documents list (new documents/signatures layer, not legacy order_documents)
units.dataPath: useDocuments()/fetchMyDocuments (NEW-layer wrapper, INT-API-PORTAL -> supabase.from('documents').select('id,display_code,title,status,generated_at,engagement_id') RLS-scoped via caller_owns_document/client_id). NOT the legacy order_documents path -- the platform documents table (§7 core.contracts). StatusBadge for DRAFT/PENDING/EXECUTED.
units.userFlow: A client opens /app/documents -> sees every contract document across their engagements with status; clicks one to view+sign (CP-PORTAL-DOC-2).
units.tests: MyDocuments.test.tsx (jsdom, useAuth mocked): render with fixture documents (one DRAFT-pending, one EXECUTED); both rows render with correct StatusBadge; clicking a pending row navigates to /app/documents/:id; assert fetchMyDocuments (the NEW documents-table wrapper) was called, NOT the legacy order_documents fn. Error + empty branches render. Static audit: rows wired to navigate; real api fn imported.

units.title: Contract view + typed-name e-sign (full client-side sign flow -> EXECUTED)
units.dataPath: fetchDocument(id) renders documents.merged_body. Sign action calls recordSignature(p_document_id,p_party_role,p_typed_name,p_ip)->rpc('record_signature') (SECURITY DEFINER). On the Nth signature the RPC flips documents.status -> EXECUTED; UI re-fetches and renders the executed confirmation.
units.userFlow: Client receives a contract -> reads the merged body -> types their legal name -> clicks Sign -> record_signature records the typed signature (sealed on sign) -> when all parties signed, status becomes EXECUTED and a confirmation renders. This is the receive->view->typed-name sign->confirmation flow.
units.tests: DocumentSign.test.tsx (jsdom, useAuth mocked): render at /app/documents/:id with a fixture pending document (merged_body present); body renders; type a name, click Sign; assert recordSignature called with EXACTLY (documentId, partyRole, typedName, ipOrNull); mock the RPC resolving with status EXECUTED -> executed confirmation renders; mock a rejection ('already signed') -> error alert renders; Sign disabled/blocked when the typed name is empty. Critical-chain UI unit (chain 2). Static audit: Sign button wired to the real RPC, no no-op.

units.title: Outstanding balance + transactions/invoice history (new billing layer)
units.dataPath: fetchMyBillableLines() -> billable_lines RLS-scoped by payer_contact_id=current_contact_id() (client_read policy); fetchMyTransactions() -> transactions RLS-scoped by payer_contact_id (INVOICE rows from settle_billable_lines). Money formats amounts. Computes outstanding = sum(OPEN billable_lines).
units.userFlow: A client opens /app/balance -> sees outstanding balance (open charges: board, lesson, consumption, fee lines) and a history of settled invoices (transactions INVOICE rows). Read-only.
units.tests: MyBalance.test.tsx (jsdom, useAuth mocked): render with fixture billable_lines (2 OPEN, 1 SETTLED) + transactions (1 INVOICE); outstanding total = sum of OPEN lines via Money; invoice history rows render; the SETTLED line is not double-counted. Error + empty branches render. Static audit: both fetches real api fns scoped by payer_contact_id; no client-side money computation bypassing the ledger.

units.title: My Horses + ownership/rights + health log (mod.horserecords client surface)
units.dataPath: useHorses()/fetchMyHorses() -> horses + horse_parties RLS-scoped via caller_owns_horse/contact_id=current_contact_id(); fetchHorseHealth(horseId) -> horse_health_events (owner reads own via caller_owns_horse). Gated behind ModuleGate('mod.horserecords') via useModules(). Shows role (owner/lessee/boarder), share_pct, and the vet/farrier/vaccination/coggins log with next_due.
units.userFlow: A client opens /app/horses -> sees each horse they own/lease/board with their role + share, and the horse's health/vet history + upcoming due dates. Hidden when the tenant lacks mod.horserecords.
units.tests: MyHorses.test.tsx (jsdom, useAuth+useModules mocked): records ON + fixture horses/horse_parties/health events -> horse cards render with correct role+share, health log rows render with next_due; expand a horse -> fetchHorseHealth called with the horse id. records OFF -> ModuleGate hides. Error branch renders. Static audit: real caller_owns_horse-scoped fetches; no dead expand.

units.title: My Board agreements + board charge history (mod.boarding client surface)
units.dataPath: fetchMyBoardAgreements() -> board_agreements RLS-scoped by boarder_contact_id=current_contact_id(); fetchMyBoardCharges() -> board_charges joined through the agreement. Gated ModuleGate('mod.boarding'). Shows rate (from registry), stall, board_type, period, and the charge history (each emits into billable_lines shown on MyBalance).
units.userFlow: A boarding client opens /app/boarding -> sees their active board agreement(s) (rate, stall, dates) and the period-by-period board charge history. Hidden when the tenant lacks mod.boarding (FHE launch: off).
units.tests: MyBoarding.test.tsx (jsdom, useAuth+useModules mocked): boarding ON + fixture board_agreements/board_charges -> agreement card renders rate/stall/status, charge rows render with period+amount (Money); boarding OFF -> ModuleGate hides. Error+empty branches render. Static audit: real boarder-scoped fetches; charges deep-link to balance, no dead nav.

units.title: My Lesson credits + bookings (mod.lessons client surface)
units.dataPath: fetchMyLessonCredits() -> lesson_credits RLS-scoped by client_id=current_client_id(); fetchMyLessonBookings() -> lesson_bookings RLS-scoped to the client. Gated ModuleGate('mod.lessons'). Shows credits_remaining/credits_total per package and the SCHEDULED/COMPLETED/CANCELLED/NO_SHOW booking history.
units.userFlow: A lesson client opens /app/lessons -> sees remaining credits per package and their booking history with status. (FHE launch tier includes mod.lessons.) Hidden when the tenant lacks mod.lessons.
units.tests: MyLessons.test.tsx (jsdom, useAuth+useModules mocked): lessons ON + fixture lesson_credits/lesson_bookings -> credit balance renders remaining/total, booking rows render with correct status badge; lessons OFF -> ModuleGate hides. Error+empty branches render. Static audit: real client-scoped fetches; no dead controls (read surface).

units.title: Per-tenant public branding consumer (slug->brand from org_public_config)
units.dataPath: useBrand() (BrandProvider hook fed by org_public_config(slug) -- the anon SECURITY DEFINER resolver returning {org_id, slug, brand:{NAME,SHORT_NAME,TAGLINE,PRIMARY_COLOR,SECONDARY_COLOR,LOGO_PATH,CONTACT_PHONE/EMAIL/URL}, modules, pricing}). useTenantSlug resolves the addressed slug from host/subdomain (or route param in SPA). Renders brand name, logo (brand-assets/{org_id}/...), colors as CSS vars, contact -- replacing hardcoded src/lib/brand.ts constants.
units.userFlow: A visitor hits tenant.example.com (or /?slug=fhe) -> the public header/hero renders THAT tenant's name, logo, colors, tagline, and contact from the registry -- no code change per tenant.
units.tests: TenantBrandHeader.test.tsx (jsdom): mock useBrand to return a fixture org_public_config brand object; render; assert brand NAME/TAGLINE render, logo src uses the org-prefixed LOGO_PATH, primary color applied as a CSS var, contact phone/email render from CONTACT_*; mock a second brand object -> header re-renders the other tenant's identity (isolation). Loading + unknown-slug (org_public_config NULL) branches render. Static audit: no hardcoded BRAND constant referenced on this path.

units.title: Per-tenant public product/service pages (registry/products, anon-scoped)
units.dataPath: org_public_config(slug).pricing (active products at current effective price, public-safe per §5.2) + org_public_config(slug).modules (which service surfaces to show). useProducts() for the authenticated path parity. Renders product/service cards (name, amount via Money) for ONLY that tenant, filtered by that tenant's active modules. Anon reads scoped by the addressed tenant (§8.6, current_addressed_org).
units.userFlow: A visitor on a tenant's public site sees THAT tenant's sellable products/services with prices from the registry, and only the module surfaces the tenant paid for. A different tenant shows a different catalog. Clicking a product routes into the intake/booking flow.
units.tests: TenantCatalog.test.tsx (jsdom): mock org_public_config -> fixture pricing[] + modules[]; render; only active products render with correct Money amounts; a product whose owning module is NOT in modules[] is hidden; a second tenant's config renders a different catalog (isolation); clicking a product calls navigate to the intake route with the product key. Empty + error branches render. Static audit: no cross-tenant catalog read; product CTA wired to a real route.

units.title: Payment -> confirmation surface on the new billing/transactions layer
units.dataPath: Reuses the existing Stripe path (startStripeCheckout -> /api/stripe-create-session; stripe-webhook marks paid) and surfaces the resulting transactions row / order status. fetchTransaction(id) or fetchOrderPayment for the confirmation. An INVOICE transaction (from settle_billable_lines) or a purchase transaction reaching PAID renders the confirmation.
units.userFlow: After a client pays (Stripe checkout or an operator marks a Zelle payment reconciled), they land on /confirmation -> the page reads the transaction/payment status and renders a paid confirmation with the amount and what it settled. The existing reconcile path stays green.
units.tests: PaymentConfirmation.test.tsx (jsdom): mock fetchTransaction/fetchOrderPayment -> a PAID fixture; render at the confirmation route; assert the paid status + amount (Money) render; mock a still-pending fixture -> pending state renders; mock a rejection -> error branch. Critical-chain 3 UI unit. Static audit: status read is a real api fn; no fabricated 'paid' state; no dead 'return to portal' link.

units.title: Transactional email /api endpoint (send-transactional) -- backend unit
units.dataPath: New Vercel function: POST /api/send-transactional-email { to, template, vars, orgId } -> resolves brand/legal footer via config_value/org_public_config (per-tenant from-name, {{ORG.*}} footer), sends via the provider (mirrors api/admin-send-invitation.ts + api/_lib), returns a message id. Server-only (api/_lib/supabaseAdmin). Uses config_value('CONTACT',...) + business_config for tenant-correct from/footer.
units.userFlow: Any transactional email (signup, contract executed, receipt, dunning) is sent tenant-branded: the correct tenant's from-name, legal footer, and contact resolve from the registry so one config write propagates to every email.
units.tests: send-transactional-email.test.ts (node env, mocked provider + mocked supabaseAdmin): assert it resolves the tenant footer via config_value with the correct org, calls the provider with the correct to/subject/tenant-branded from, returns success; a provider failure returns 5xx and does NOT throw uncaught; a missing 'to' -> 400. Static audit: no hardcoded FHE from-address; org-scoped resolution.

units.title: Document delivery on EXECUTED (document_deliveries + email) -- backend unit
units.dataPath: New function: POST /api/deliver-document { documentId } -> when documents.status='EXECUTED', writes a document_deliveries row per party (recipient_contact_id, channel, copy_url) and calls send-transactional-email with the executed-contract template + tenant brand. Idempotent per (document_id, recipient_contact_id, channel). Reads engagement parties for recipients. Tables: documents, document_deliveries, engagement_parties, signatures (§15 chain 2 tail).
units.userFlow: When a contract reaches EXECUTED (all signatures recorded), each party is emailed their executed copy and a document_deliveries row is recorded -- completing the sign->EXECUTED->deliver->email tail.
units.tests: deliver-document.test.ts (node env): mocked admin client returning an EXECUTED document + 2 parties; assert 2 document_deliveries rows inserted with correct recipient_contact_id/channel/copy_url and send-transactional-email called per party with the tenant-correct template; a non-EXECUTED document is rejected (no premature delivery); re-invocation is idempotent (no duplicate deliveries). Static audit: delivery only fires on EXECUTED; no orphan delivery without an email attempt.

units.title: INTEGRATION: /api/admin-provision-tenant edge function (find-or-create ADMIN auth user -> provision_tenant)
units.dataPath: New Vercel function mirroring api/admin-send-invitation.ts: authorize the caller as SUPER_ADMIN, find-or-create the ADMIN auth user by email via the Supabase Auth admin API (idempotent), then rpc('provision_tenant',{p_name,p_slug,p_tier_key,p_admin_email,p_admin_user_id,p_brand,p_legal,p_rates,p_modules}) in one txn; returns {org_id}. Orphan-safe (idempotent find-or-create + re-runnable RPC, §9). The server half the SUPERADMIN-PROVISION wizard's provisionTenant wrapper posts to.
units.userFlow: Not a screen. The blessed provisioning entry point behind the wizard; assisted-onboarding now, self-serve later reuses the same RPC (§9).
units.tests: admin-provision-tenant.test.ts (node env, mocked supabase admin client): (a) a valid request find-or-creates the auth user then calls rpc('provision_tenant') with the exact payload and returns the org_id; (b) re-running with the same email does NOT create a second user (idempotent); (c) a non-superadmin caller is rejected 403; (d) an RPC error surfaces as a clean error, no orphaned partial. §15 chain-1 assertion. Existing api tests + typecheck:api green.

units.title: INTEGRATION: api.ts portal/public read wrappers + intake + form_definitions + brand runtime path
units.dataPath: SHARED FILE src/lib/api.ts -- runs SERIALLY after INT-API-MODULES, gated by dependsOn on every portal/public UI slice whose wrapper it lands (dependsOn is the only collision guard, §12). MUST NOT re-author the already-present myModules/orgPublicConfig/configValue/provisionTenant. Adds: fetchFormDefinition, submitIntake/submitClientIntake, fetchMyEngagements/fetchEngagement/fetchEngagementDocuments, fetchMyDocuments/fetchDocument (NEW documents table), fetchMyBillableLines/fetchMyTransactions, fetchMyHorses/fetchHorseHealth, fetchMyBoardAgreements/fetchMyBoardCharges, fetchMyLessonCredits/fetchMyLessonBookings, fetchTransaction/fetchOrderPayment. brand.ts gains a runtime org_public_config fetch path (constant kept as FHE prerender fallback per §12).
units.userFlow: N/A (data layer). Every portal/public screen's data fn resolves to a real Supabase RPC/table call with tenant/role scoping.
units.tests: api.portal.test.ts (mocked supabase client, §15.2): recordSignature already covered in INT-API-CORE; fetchMyDocuments selects from 'documents' (NOT order_documents); fetchMyBillableLines selects billable_lines; fetchFormDefinition selects form_definitions by form_key+active. Static audit: every exported wrapper imported by >=1 slice; no defined-but-uncalled wrapper; no RPC name typo (assert against the migration RPC list). Single serial edit site for api.ts's portal additions.

units.title: INTEGRATION: App.tsx ops+portal+public routing (every /app/ops/** + portal + public route under role/member-gated ProtectedRoute + OpsRouteGuard)
units.dataPath: SHARED FILE src/App.tsx + new OpsRouteGuard. Adds nested <Route> children under the existing /app ProtectedRoute for every ops/admin/superadmin page AND the new portal routes (/app/intake, /app/engagements, /app/engagements/:id, /app/documents, /app/documents/:id, /app/balance, /app/horses, /app/boarding, /app/lessons) + public tenant catalog/intake + /confirmation. OpsRouteGuard wraps module/role-gated routes with useModules()/role checks (redirect or lock when absent) -- server RLS/require_module is the real fence; Layer C convenience. Serial (shared App.tsx), after all pages exist.
units.userFlow: Not a screen. Wires each page to its URL so nav links resolve. A module-off route renders the lock/redirect, not a crash.
units.tests: App.routes.test.tsx (jsdom): mock useModules/role; render the router at representative routes (/app/ops, /app/ops/engagements/new, /app/ops/barnops/resources, /app/documents, /app/balance, /app/ops/superadmin/provision) and assert the right page mounts; a route whose module is OFF renders the OpsRouteGuard lock/redirect (not the page); a non-admin at an admin route is blocked. Static audit: no route points at a missing component; existing routes unchanged/green.

units.title: INTEGRATION: AppLayout ops+portal nav (module/role-gated nav section for ops/admin/superadmin + client portal)
units.dataPath: SHARED FILE src/components/app/AppLayout.tsx + new OpsNav. Adds an OpsNav block driven by useModules()/role: core ops links (Dashboard, Contacts, Horses, Engagements, Documents, Intake, Transactions) for staff; module links (Boarding, Inventory, Lessons, Records, Employees) only when has_module; admin links (Modules, Registry, Branding, Products) for ADMIN; Provision for SUPER_ADMIN. Also adds client-portal nav entries (My Engagements/Documents/Balance/Horses/Boarding/Lessons) gated by useModules() for the member. Serial (shared AppLayout.tsx), after INT-AUTH/INT-ROUTES.
units.userFlow: Not a screen. Staff see an Ops section; clients see portal links; only entitled modules + role-appropriate admin/superadmin links appear. Clicking navigates to the INT-ROUTES routes.
units.tests: AppLayout.ops-nav.test.tsx (jsdom): mock useModules->{mod.lessons:true,mod.boarding:false}, role=ADMIN; render, assert core ops + Lessons links present, Boarding link ABSENT (gated), admin links present, Provision absent (not super); switch role=SUPER_ADMIN -> Provision appears; a member client sees My Engagements (brokerage on) but not My Boarding (boarding off). Static audit: every nav link targets a real INT-ROUTES route; existing member nav unchanged/green.

units.title: INTEGRATION: wire /api email + delivery + provision routes (vercel.json/package.json)
units.dataPath: SHARED FILES vercel.json + package.json -- serial integration touch registering the three new backend functions (send-transactional-email, deliver-document, admin-provision-tenant) as deployable /api routes and any build/test script additions, so they are reachable in production. No new data path; wires existing backend units. dependsOn is the only collision guard on the shared config files (§12).
units.userFlow: N/A (deployment wiring). The transactional-email, document-delivery, and provision endpoints become live routes.
units.tests: api/routes.test.ts: assert vercel.json (or the framework's function discovery) exposes /api/send-transactional-email, /api/deliver-document, /api/admin-provision-tenant; assert package.json test/build scripts include the new api test files. Static audit: no orphaned function file that is never routed; no route pointing at a missing handler.

units.title: CRITICAL CHAIN 1 E2E: provision-tenant -> login -> gated nav + branded public site
units.dataPath: End-to-end trace across /api/admin-provision-tenant -> provision_tenant() -> org + registry (business_config/config_values) + org_modules + ADMIN profile, THEN: org_public_config(slug) returns the new tenant's brand (public site branded), my_modules() returns the tier's modules, and a login renders the correctly gated nav. Two layers: a node api test proving provision_tenant lands the rows (PGlite), and a jsdom test proving the resulting brand+modules drive the UI. RPC/tables: provision_tenant, org_modules, config_values, organizations, org_public_config, my_modules (§15 chain 1).
units.userFlow: Operator provisions a new tenant (name, slug, tier, admin email) -> the tenant boots with a branded public site and, on first ADMIN login, the nav shows exactly the tier's module surfaces. Re-runnable/idempotent by email.
units.tests: Node PGlite portion: call provision_tenant with a fixture (slug 'boarding-barn' to avoid the existing 'rival' collision, tier.boarding) as SUPER_ADMIN; assert org_modules matches the tier (boarding/horserecords/barnops present, brokerage ABSENT), config_values seeded, ADMIN profile created; re-run -> idempotent (no dup). jsdom portion: mock org_public_config + my_modules with the provisioned tenant's config; render the public header (asserts the new tenant's brand, not FHE's) and the logged-in nav (asserts boarding nav present, engagements gated out). Independent skeptic re-runs the trace.

units.title: CRITICAL CHAIN 2 E2E: intake -> engagement -> generate -> sign -> EXECUTED -> deliver -> email
units.dataPath: Full contract chain (§15 chain 2): public intake (anon, addressed tenant, requests row scoped by slug->org) -> engagement (create_*_engagement) -> generate_document (keyed to v_eng.org_id) producing merged_body with the ADDRESSED tenant's {{ORG.*}}/{{FHE.SIGNATORY_NAME}} -> record_signature xN -> documents.status='EXECUTED' -> /api/deliver-document writes document_deliveries + sends email. Node PGlite proves the data chain lands tenant-correct; jsdom proves the client-side receive->view->sign->confirmation UI drives record_signature and renders EXECUTED.
units.userFlow: A prospect submits a public intake on tenant A -> an engagement + contract are generated with A's legal identity (never B's) -> the client reviews and types their name to sign -> all parties signed -> status EXECUTED -> each party is emailed their copy. Tenant-correct end to end.
units.tests: Node PGlite portion: seed tenant A+B with distinct business_config.signatory_name; run the chain for an A engagement; assert generate_document merged_body contains A's signatory and NOT B's (isolation, §6.2); record_signature to EXECUTED; assert deliver-document inserts document_deliveries per party and calls the email fn. jsdom portion: render DocumentSign with the generated fixture, type name, click Sign; assert recordSignature called with correct args and the EXECUTED confirmation renders (success) + a rejection renders the error. Independent-skeptic re-runnable; closes §15 chain 2 including the deliver->email tail.

units.title: CRITICAL CHAIN 3 E2E: payment -> mark-paid -> confirm (existing Stripe/Zelle path stays green)
units.dataPath: The existing Stripe/Zelle reconcile path (startStripeCheckout -> /api/stripe-create-session; stripe-webhook / zelle-reconcile mark paid) reaching a transactions/payments PAID state, surfaced by fetchTransaction/fetchOrderPayment on the new billing layer (§15 chain 3). Proves the transaction reaches PAID and the confirmation renders; the existing reconcile tests stay green (this is additive, does not re-author the /api reconcile path).
units.userFlow: A client pays (Stripe or operator-reconciled Zelle) -> the transaction reaches PAID -> /confirmation reads the status and renders a paid confirmation with the amount. The existing reconcile path stays green.
units.tests: paymentConfirm.e2e.test.tsx (jsdom): mock fetchTransaction/fetchOrderPayment resolving a PAID fixture that mirrors the shape stripe-webhook/zelle-reconcile stamp; render PaymentConfirmation; assert the paid status + amount (Money) render; a pending fixture -> pending state; a rejection -> error branch. Asserts the existing reconcile api contract is unchanged (imports the real fetchOrderPayment, no fabricated 'paid'). Independent-skeptic re-runnable; closes §15 chain 3.

units.title: CRITICAL CHAIN 4 E2E: consumption -> cost-allocation -> billable_line -> invoice on portal
units.dataPath: The client-facing tail of §15 chain 4: consumption_events -> resolve_consumption_billing() (split from horse_parties, override via cost_allocation_rules) -> billable_lines per payer -> settle_billable_lines() -> transactions INVOICE. Verifies the PORTAL correctly SURFACES the outcome: MyBalance reads the resulting billable_lines (open) and, after settlement, the transactions INVOICE, scoped by payer_contact_id. The RPC determinism is owned by the schema tests; this proves the client sees the right numbers.
units.userFlow: After the barn logs consumption and runs cost allocation + settlement, the payer client opens /app/balance and sees their correctly-attributed charges and, once settled, the invoice -- proving cost_allocations actually reach the client's invoice view.
units.tests: Node PGlite portion (mod.barnops on): log a consumption_event on a leased horse with horse_parties owner/lessee split, run resolve_consumption_billing, assert billable_lines per payer with correct shares; run settle_billable_lines, assert a transactions INVOICE = SUM and lines SETTLED. jsdom portion: mock fetchMyBillableLines/fetchMyTransactions with the resolved rows for the lessee payer; render MyBalance; assert the lessee's outstanding reflects ONLY their share and the settled invoice appears in history. Error branch renders. Independent-skeptic re-runnable; closes the client-visible end of chain 4.
